YTD ACV: $1.3M
Projected Consumed: $2.2M
Projected Budget: $2.5M
Projected ACV: $3.1M
EA Insights and Actions
No insights available
Solution Areas
Solution Area
BAIP - Data & AI
89.7%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$53K
YTD Budget$59K
YTD ACV: $66K
Projected Consumed: $128K · Projected Budget: $166K · Projected ACV: $159K
Solution Insights and Actions
The Data & AI foundation is inverted — SAC BI is consuming at near-budget pace ($47,638 YTD, 94.5% attainment) while Datasphere underneath it sits flat at ~$1,113/month, meaning Lubrizol is visualizing data through SAC without leveraging the unified semantic layer Datasphere is architected to provide, leaving dashboards likely sourced from point connections rather than a governed, AI-ready data fabric.
Datasphere is deployed as a single steady-state workload rather than an enterprise data foundation — the flat $1,044–$1,218 monthly band signals one pipeline in production with no expansion into new domains, which directly undermines the $25,825 Sep–Dec ramp and leaves downstream SAC BI and AI workloads without the federated, semantically-rich data layer they depend on.
Run a joint SAC BI–Datasphere architecture review to reroute existing SAC stories onto Datasphere models, converting the dormant semantic layer into the governed source for the analytics already in active use.
Launch a Datasphere data product expansion workshop to onboard 2–3 new source systems and business domains, addressing the flat consumption ceiling and absorbing the back-loaded $25,825 budget through genuine landscape unification rather than isolated workload growth.
Solution Area
BAIP - Technology Foundation
177.6%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$108K
YTD Budget$61K
YTD ACV: $90K
Projected Consumed: $259K · Projected Budget: $177K · Projected ACV: $216K
Solution Insights and Actions
The Technology Foundation tier is architecturally inverted at Lubrizol — Integration Suite (the API/AI-agent connectivity backbone) and its underlying BTP consumption are running 2-5x over plan while SAP Build (the low-code extension layer that should ride on top of those integrations) is flat-lined at 33% attainment, meaning Lubrizol is wiring systems together aggressively but not building the business-facing apps and workflows that convert that integration spend into LoB productivity.
The Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement is functioning as an uncontrolled shared substrate — Joule Studio workloads are 5-6x over plan and Integration Suite workloads are 61% over plan on the same LPR868 envelope, indicating BTP is being consumed without service-level governance or chargeback discipline and the original sizing no longer reflects how the foundation is actually being used.
Run a unified BTP consumption deep-dive across LPR868 (Joule Studio and Integration Suite lanes) and LPR499 to attribute the $100K+ projected overrun to specific iFlows, APIs, and AI workloads, then resize both agreements against the real $240K+ run rate before ceiling breach forces throttling.
Launch an SAP Build activation program tied to the live Integration Suite workloads — use the newly productive APIs as the foundation for 2-3 citizen-developer apps and process automations so the dormant low-code layer starts monetizing the integration investment instead of leaving $15K of committed Build value stranded.
Solution Area
Business Transformation Management
57.6%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$20K
YTD Budget$35K
YTD ACV: $102K
Projected Consumed: $48K · Projected Budget: $121K · Projected ACV: $245K
Solution Insights and Actions
The Signavio process transformation stack is architecturally split-brained at Lubrizol — Process Management entitlement scaled 5x to $48K ACV in May while Process Intelligence remains pinned at a flat $1,227/month, meaning the LoB is licensing capacity to model and govern processes faster than it is mining real execution data to know which processes deserve that attention.
Process Management's 40.7% YTD attainment against a back-half budget ramp to $14K/month indicates entitlement was procured ahead of an activation plan — process owners, modeling conventions, and a target use case portfolio (likely SOX or S/4 transformation prep) have not been operationalized, so the LoB owns a process repository capability it is not yet using to drive transformation outcomes.
Convene a joint Signavio Process Intelligence and Process Management value review to tie expanded LPR1004 entitlement to specific event-log-backed investigations in LPR1123, closing the architectural disconnect between process discovery and process governance.
Launch a Process Management activation sprint that onboards named process owners against two to three anchor use cases (SOX controls documentation, S/4 transformation readiness) to absorb the $20,124 YTD and projected $60K annual consumption gap on LPR1004.
Solution Area
Customer Experience
16.3%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$14K
YTD Budget$83K
YTD ACV: $161K
Projected Consumed: $32K · Projected Budget: $204K · Projected ACV: $387K
Solution Insights and Actions
SAP Commerce Cloud is architecturally positioned as Lubrizol's unified B2B/B2C digital commerce backbone — yet the flatlined consumption pattern indicates only a single narrow workload (likely one storefront or catalog) is live, leaving core capabilities like B2B self-service, promotions, order management, and personalized catalogs dormant in the Customer Experience LoB.
With ~84% of contracted Commerce Cloud value projected to go unused against a $203K annual budget, the platform is functioning as a static transactional endpoint rather than the omnichannel engine it was procured to be — meaning Lubrizol is paying for unified commerce architecture while operating in single-channel mode, with no AI-driven personalization or cross-channel insight loop activated.
Conduct a SAP Commerce Cloud capability audit with Lubrizol's digital commerce team to map which storefronts, B2B buyer portals, and product catalogs remain unonboarded and quantify the workload expansion needed to close the $14K monthly budget gap.
Define a phased SAP Commerce Cloud activation roadmap targeting the $171K residual ACV by sequencing B2B self-service portals, catalog expansion, and AI-driven personalization to move consumption beyond the current $2,700 plateau.
Solution Area
Finance and Spend Management
99.7%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$454K
YTD Budget$455K
YTD ACV: $528K
Projected Consumed: $1.1M · Projected Budget: $1.1M · Projected ACV: $1.3M
Solution Insights and Actions
Lubrizol's Source-to-Pay architecture is operating with a structural imbalance — transactional layers (Ariba Buying and Invoicing at 110%+ attainment, ExpenseIt at 125%+, Reporting Services at 129%) are systematically over-consuming while the upstream strategic layer (Ariba Sourcing at 61% YTD, Ariba Catalog frozen at 6%) is dormant, meaning the LoB is processing high transaction volumes without the sourcing pipeline or catalog governance needed to channel that spend into negotiated value.
The Travel & Expense estate is showing a consistent capacity-undersizing pattern across every Concur SKU (Expense, ExpenseIt, Reporting Services, Other, Preferred Success, and Document & Reporting Compliance all tracking 125–849% of plan), indicating the original commercial envelope was sized against a pre-adoption baseline and the LoB is now operating a fully embedded compliance-and-expense platform against a contract that no longer reflects its run-rate.
Trigger a consolidated true-up across Ariba Buying and Invoicing (+$52K), Concur Expense (+$4.7K), ExpenseIt (+$1.7K), Reporting Services (+$5K), Preferred Success Concur (+$3.2K), Concur Other (+$20.5K), and Document & Reporting Compliance public to align contracted ceilings with sustained over-consumption before multiple ACVs breach simultaneously.
Reactivate the strategic procurement layer by launching an Ariba Catalog supplier enablement and buyer redirection program tied to an Ariba Sourcing event pipeline review, converting the combined ~$73K of unrealized sourcing and catalog ACV into governed, catalog-driven spend that feeds the already-saturated Ariba Buying and Invoicing transactional flow.
Solution Area
Human Capital Management
92.9%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$280K
YTD Budget$301K
YTD ACV: $329K
Projected Consumed: $672K · Projected Budget: $731K · Projected ACV: $790K
Solution Insights and Actions
Lubrizol's HCM architecture is functionally complete on paper but operationally bifurcated — Employee Central (the core HR system of record) is locked at a flat $12,626/month baseline while every downstream talent module (Compensation, Performance, Career Development, Onboarding) is either eroding or stuck below 90% attainment, meaning the foundational data layer is not driving the integrated talent processes it was architected to feed.
Preferred Success SFSF is saturated at the $10,575 ACV ceiling while the talent suite it is meant to accelerate is collectively under-consuming by ~$60K projected annually — advisory and expert capacity is being consumed faster than contracted, yet that guidance is not translating into expanded adoption of Career Development, Onboarding, or Compensation, signalling the success engagement is firefighting rather than driving module activation.
Launch an Employee Central-anchored talent activation program that uses EC position, org, and skills data to light up the dormant Career and Talent Development pathing, Performance goal cascades, and Compensation planning worksheets as one connected hire-to-grow-to-reward flow rather than isolated modules.
Expand the Preferred Success SFSF entitlement to cover the projected $17,037 overage and redirect the advisory hours specifically toward Onboarding template rollout and Compensation cycle re-anchoring, where adoption erosion is steepest and architectural value loss is highest.
Solution Area
Supply Chain Management
100.0%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$6K
YTD Budget$6K
YTD ACV: $6K
Projected Consumed: $14K · Projected Budget: $16K · Projected ACV: $14K
Solution Insights and Actions
Lubrizol's Supply Chain Management footprint on SAP is architecturally narrow — Business Network Commerce Automation is the sole active capability, meaning supplier collaboration and electronic document exchange are running, but there is no adjacent sourcing, contract, or supply planning product in the signal set to govern upstream decisions or downstream fulfillment.
The flat $1,193 monthly run-rate on Business Network Commerce Automation indicates an embedded but static transaction flow — the automation is operational for a fixed supplier and document scope, but the back-half budget escalation to $1,517+ reveals SAP expected document volume or supplier enablement to expand, and that growth in the buyer-supplier network is not materializing.
Expand Business Network Commerce Automation supplier enablement and transaction categories (PO confirmations, ship notices, invoice flows) with Lubrizol to absorb the ~$325/month step-up and convert the static workflow into a growing collaboration network.
Clarify whether the $1,193 monthly Business Network Commerce Automation charge is a fixed subscription floor or variable transaction-based consumption, since that determines whether supplier onboarding levers can actually move consumption against the $15,641 projected annual budget.
Tropicana Products, Inc.
Consumer products
YTD Consumed: $427K
YTD Budget: $515K
YTD ACV: $495K
Projected Consumed: $1.0M
Projected Budget: $1.3M
Projected ACV: $1.2M
EA Insights and Actions
Tropicana has contracted Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreements in both Data & AI (Database Cloud) and Finance (LPR868) yet both sit at $0 consumption, meaning the persistence and extensibility foundation beneath Datasphere, SAC BI, and SAC Planning is contractually present but architecturally absent — every analytics and planning workload above it is running on borrowed time without the unified data layer the contracts were sized to deliver.
The Integration Suite five-month decline is the single architectural fault line connecting Tropicana's simultaneous deterioration in Datasphere pipelines, SAC BI dashboards, Concur Expense workflow collapse, dormant Multi Bank Connect, and missing IBP-to-Integrated Product Development handoffs — the connective tissue is failing, so every cross-domain process (plan-to-make, record-to-report, source-to-pay, hire-to-retire) is degrading in parallel rather than as isolated incidents.
Across Data & AI, Supply Chain, Finance, and Business Transformation, Tropicana shows a consistent pattern of contracted-but-dormant foundational layers (Database CPEA, IBP, Multi Bank Connect, Integrated Product Development, Signavio Process Management governance) paired with declining usage in the consumption layers above them, indicating an enterprise that bought a complete digital core blueprint but is operating it as disconnected point tools — blocking the consumer-goods agility, new-product-introduction speed, and integrated planning outcomes the architecture was designed to enable.
Convene a cross-domain Platform Foundation Activation Review covering both Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreements (Database Cloud and LPR868), BTP, and Joule Studio to land first production workloads that simultaneously serve Datasphere persistence and SAC Planning extensibility, closing the combined ~$4,500+ foundational gap with architecturally linked use cases.
Commission an Integration Suite-led Process Backbone Workshop that maps the dormant iFlows to the broken cross-domain chains — Datasphere pipelines, Concur-to-S/4 expense posting, Multi Bank Connect-to-Treasury, and IBP-to-Integrated Product Development — and rebuilds them as a single integration roadmap rather than five isolated remediations.
Establish a Tropicana Enterprise Center of Excellence anchored on Signavio Process Management and Process Intelligence that owns the end-to-end process inventory across Finance, Supply Chain, and R&D, converting the $1,489 expanded Process Management ACV into the governance layer that drives adoption of IBP, Integrated Product Development, Concur, and SAC Planning as a connected operating model.
Solution Areas
Solution Area
BAIP - Data & AI
96.5%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$113K
YTD Budget$118K
YTD ACV: $91K
Projected Consumed: $272K · Projected Budget: $294K · Projected ACV: $217K
Solution Insights and Actions
Tropicana's Data & AI foundation is architecturally inverted — the Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement on Database Cloud sits at $0 consumed against $3,220 YTD budget while the BDC Public CPEA runs 9.8% above plan, meaning the persistence and database layer underpinning Datasphere and Data Intelligence has never been activated even as compute-side workloads accelerate, leaving the data architecture dependent on a foundation that is contracted but not productive.
The semantic and integration layer is quietly eroding — Datasphere and Data Intelligence Cloud have both declined for two to three consecutive months while SAC BI consumption has fallen 27% since February, indicating that as fewer pipelines land trusted data into the unified layer, downstream analytics consumers are disengaging, so the LoB is losing the AI-ready, business-context data fabric the BDC stack was designed to deliver.
Trigger an activation workshop for the Database Cloud Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement to land a first persistence or data service workload and bring the dormant foundation under Datasphere and Data Intelligence into productive use.
Launch a joint Datasphere and Data Intelligence Cloud pipeline audit to identify the paused replication flows or stalled jobs behind the April–May decline, then rebuild SAC BI's top dashboards on those restored semantic models to reconnect reporting to recurring business decisions.
Solution Area
BAIP - Technology Foundation
64.7%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$17K
YTD Budget$27K
YTD ACV: $0
Projected Consumed: $42K · Projected Budget: $66K · Projected ACV: $0
Solution Insights and Actions
The Technology Foundation layer at Tropicana is architecturally provisioned across three distinct planes — Joule Studio for AI extensibility, Integration Suite for process connectivity, and general BTP services — yet all three show the same contracting or flatlined signature, meaning the platform exists as a capability shell without the workloads needed to deliver foundational value to the LoB.
Integration Suite is the most consequential gap because its five-month consumption decline signals that the connective tissue between SAP and non-SAP systems is weakening, which architecturally undermines every downstream extension, AI, and data scenario that depends on integrated process flows — including the Joule Studio and broader BTP investments that are themselves underused.
Re-anchor Integration Suite as the backbone of Tropicana's Technology Foundation by identifying the dormant iFlows and APIs behind the $9,920 projected annual shortfall and rebuilding an integration roadmap tied to active S/4 and line-of-business process needs.
Convert Joule Studio and the broader BTP footprint from single-workload pilots into a multi-scenario adoption plan by pairing each new Integration Suite flow with a corresponding extension or AI use case, closing the combined ~$12,800 platform shortfall through architecturally linked workloads rather than isolated activations.
Solution Area
Business Transformation Management
52.1%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$55K
YTD Budget$105K
YTD ACV: $123K
Projected Consumed: $131K · Projected Budget: $257K · Projected ACV: $296K
Solution Insights and Actions
The Business Transformation Management stack at Tropicana is architecturally bifurcated — Signavio Process Intelligence (the mining/diagnostic layer) is contracted at scale but trending to leave ~49% of value unused, while Process Management (the modeling/governance layer) is being consumed reactively in project bursts, meaning the LoB has the tooling to discover process friction but lacks the embedded modeling discipline to redesign and govern what mining reveals.
The May -$18,530 reversal on Process Intelligence combined with the volatile Process Management pattern (flat ~$140, spike to $549, drop to $207) signals that transformation work is being run as discrete consulting-style engagements rather than as a continuous Center of Excellence capability, which is why the expanded $1,489 ACV on Process Management has no matching user base or process inventory to absorb it.
Validate the SAP Signavio Process Intelligence May -$18,530 negative posting with billing to confirm whether it is a credit memo or data error, then launch a process mining expansion sprint onboarding two to three additional in-scope processes to recover the $125,941 projected annual gap.
Stand up a Signavio Center of Excellence with a user activation and process inventory program for SAP Signavio Process Management to convert the expanded $1,489 ACV into steady monthly consumption and close the $403 projected over-consumption gap through governed modeling rather than reactive project use.
Solution Area
Finance and Spend Management
63.4%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$37K
YTD Budget$59K
YTD ACV: $73K
Projected Consumed: $89K · Projected Budget: $152K · Projected ACV: $175K
Solution Insights and Actions
Tropicana's Finance and Spend Management architecture is fragmented at the foundation — Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement sits at $0 consumption while SAC Planning runs as an isolated, static planning footprint, meaning the LoB has no unified data and extensibility layer beneath its analytics investment and cannot scale planning into operational domains as the architecture intends.
The Travel and Expense capability is architecturally contracted but operationally collapsing — Concur Expense attainment fell from 83% to 21% while ExpenseIt mobile capture is flat near $290/month and Reporting Services shows no signal, meaning Tropicana has the full T&E stack on paper but is losing the enterprise-standard expense workflow, the mobile capture feeder, and the analytics tier that justify the investment.
Treasury and Working Capital is dormant — Multi Bank Connect shows no adoption signal, leaving SAP S/4HANA treasury without its intended single connectivity hub to banks and forcing the LoB to rely on point-to-point bank integrations that the product was contracted to eliminate.
Financial Planning & Analysis is delivering reporting inertia rather than forward-looking decision support — SAC Planning consumption is flat-to-declining ($3,020 to $2,861) and projects a $6,058 annual shortfall, signaling a single static planning model rather than the unified financial and operational planning the architecture is designed to enable.
Activate Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement (LPR868) by launching an onboarding workshop to land a first production workload — ideally one that extends SAC Planning data integration — to close the $625 YTD and $1,366 annual gap.
Restore Concur Expense (LPR898) as the enterprise standard at Tropicana by enforcing a no-reimbursement-outside-Concur policy, reactivating lapsed submitters, and pairing rollout with ExpenseIt (LPR950) mobile capture and Reporting Services (LPR1091) activation to recover the $45,139 at-risk annual value and re-engage the full T&E stack.
Stand up Multi Bank Connect (LPR380) with a bank-onboarding plan covering payment initiation, statement retrieval, and SWIFT gpi tracking to eliminate point-to-point integrations and activate the dormant Treasury & Working Capital capability.
Expand SAP Analytics Cloud Planning (LPR001) beyond its static finance model by onboarding a second planning domain such as workforce or sales planning to break the $2,861 consumption ceiling and close the projected $6,058 annual gap.
Solution Area
Supply Chain Management
99.1%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$205K
YTD Budget$207K
YTD ACV: $209K
Projected Consumed: $491K · Projected Budget: $496K · Projected ACV: $501K
Solution Insights and Actions
Tropicana's Supply Chain Management architecture is fundamentally incomplete — Integrated Product Development is contracted to bridge design, cost, and supply feasibility, but with consumption decaying to $59 in May and tracking to ~16% of the $5,569 annual budget, the new product introduction lane is effectively dormant, leaving R&D handoffs to manufacturing and procurement happening outside SAP.
The Plan-to-Make value chain is architecturally disconnected — IBP is meant to translate demand signals into supply response and feed feasibility back into product design via Integrated Product Development, but with no adoption signals on IBP and Integrated Product Development in decline, Tropicana is running portfolio and S&OP decisions without the unified planning backbone the contract was designed to deliver.
Anchor Integrated Product Development to a named R&D owner at Tropicana and activate formula management, specification handoff, and BOM collaboration scenarios to close the $4,658 projected shortfall and reverse the declining consumption trend.
Stand up the IBP demand sensing and S&OP process with supply chain planning leadership to establish the forecasting and response planning foundation that Integrated Product Development depends on for cost and feasibility alignment.
VS Service Company, LLC
Professional services
YTD Consumed: $276K
YTD Budget: $299K
YTD ACV: $257K
Projected Consumed: $663K
Projected Budget: $711K
Projected ACV: $616K
EA Insights and Actions
The Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement is simultaneously over-consumed in Technology Foundation (Joule Studio at 133%, Integration Suite at 113%) and Data & AI (Database Cloud in sustained overage) while sitting completely dormant in Finance and Spend Management and in the BDC Public bucket — meaning the same BTP contract is structurally undersized where it matters most and shelfware where it should be wiring procurement into the data fabric, fragmenting what should be one coherent extensibility platform into disconnected islands of overrun and waste.
VS Service Company has activated the transactional layers of every domain (Ariba Buying/Invoicing, SAP Business Data Cloud, Joule Studio, Integration Suite) but left the upstream governance and orchestration layers inert (Ariba Sourcing at 29%, BDC Public at 9.5%, Technology Foundation Other at 0%), producing an enterprise architecture that processes work but neither sources it competitively nor extends it intelligently — eroding the savings, compliance, and AI-leverage outcomes the suite was designed to deliver.
Across the portfolio the customer is on track for ~$27K+ of uncontracted overage in Technology Foundation and Data & AI while simultaneously carrying ~$42K of unconsumed BDC Public balance and a dormant CPEA in Finance — a net architectural mis-allocation where the LoB is paying twice (overage on hot workloads, shelfware on cold ones) instead of rebalancing a single platform envelope to match real demand.
Convene a single cross-domain CPEA architecture review spanning Data & AI, Technology Foundation, and Finance to rebalance the contracted envelope — true-up the overage on Joule Studio, Integration Suite, and Database Cloud against the unconsumed BDC Public and Finance CPEA balances before fiscal year-end exposure compounds.
Run an integration coherence workshop to wire Ariba Sourcing and Buying/Invoicing data into SAP Business Data Cloud via Integration Suite, simultaneously activating the dormant Finance CPEA, restoring closed-loop source-to-pay governance, and giving the BDC data fabric a procurement-grade workload to anchor.
Stand up a named-workload activation plan for the inert scopes — Technology Foundation Other, CPEA BDC Public, and Finance CPEA — assigning LoB owners and first production use cases so contracted-but-unlaunched capacity converts into adoption rather than expiring as shelfware.
Solution Areas
Solution Area
BAIP - Data & AI
86.9%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$34K
YTD Budget$39K
YTD ACV: $53K
Projected Consumed: $81K · Projected Budget: $96K · Projected ACV: $128K
Solution Insights and Actions
The Data & AI foundation is structurally under-sized for actual workload demand — SAP Business Data Cloud is running at 2x its contracted envelope ($24.5K YTD vs $12.4K budget, full-year ACV of $10.6K already exceeded by $13.8K) while Database Cloud on the CPEA is also in sustained 6–24% monthly overage, indicating the LoB has operationalized the business data fabric and is deriving real value, but the commercial sizing no longer reflects the embedded adoption footprint.
The broader BDC Public platform capability under CPEA is effectively dormant after a single January spike (9.5% YTD attainment, ~$42K of contracted value at risk of going unused), meaning VS Service Company is consuming the mission-critical data and database layers of the Data & AI stack but not the surrounding extension, integration, and AI-anchoring services — leaving SAP Business Data Cloud's agentic AI and analytics capabilities architecturally available but functionally isolated from the rest of the platform.
Initiate a coordinated true-up across SAP Business Data Cloud and the CPEA Database Cloud bucket to realign ACV with the demonstrated ~$4.9K/month BDC run-rate and close the projected $1.1K Database Cloud ceiling breach before overage exposure compounds.
Build a CPEA BDC Public adoption plan that activates recurring extension, integration, or data-product workloads against the $41.9K unconsumed balance, anchoring them to the already-active SAP Business Data Cloud data fabric so the platform investment converts into sustained Data & AI capability rather than shelfware.
Solution Area
BAIP - Technology Foundation
120.4%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$64K
YTD Budget$53K
YTD ACV: $0
Projected Consumed: $154K · Projected Budget: $127K · Projected ACV: $0
Solution Insights and Actions
The Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement is the connective tissue underneath Joule Studio and SAP Integration Suite, and both workloads are running hot (133% and 113% attainment respectively) — the LoB is architecturally committed to AI-assisted extensibility and integration but operating without a sized ceiling, meaning production-grade capabilities are being consumed on an undersized contract that will breach before fiscal year end.
Technology Foundation Other sits at $0 consumed against a back-loaded $107 plan with 96% of budget concentrated in Jun–Aug, indicating a contracted-but-unlaunched foundational capability — every month of delay collapses an already narrow ramp window and signals the LoB has no named workload owner driving activation.
Initiate a true-up on the Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement covering Joule Studio and Integration Suite to expand the contracted ceiling against the combined ~$26,800 projected annual overage and attach consumption monitoring thresholds to prevent further uncontrolled overrun.
Stand up a Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement activation workshop for the dormant Technology Foundation Other scope, naming workloads and owners to convert the front-loaded Jun–Aug capacity into billable consumption before the ramp window closes.
Solution Area
Finance and Spend Management
86.2%
YTD Budget Attainment
YTD Consumed$179K
YTD Budget$207K
YTD ACV: $203K
Projected Consumed: $428K · Projected Budget: $488K · Projected ACV: $488K
Solution Insights and Actions
The source-to-pay architecture is operating in its downstream half only — Ariba Buying and Invoicing is processing transactions while Ariba Sourcing sits at 29% attainment with a declining event cadence, meaning VS Service Company is paying invoices for goods and services that were never competitively sourced, eroding the savings capture the suite was designed to deliver.
The Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement remains architecturally contracted but operationally inert at $0 consumption, leaving the extensibility and integration fabric that should connect Ariba transactional data to broader finance and planning workloads dormant — the LoB has no platform layer activated to support analytics, automation, or custom workflows on top of its procurement investments.
Invoicing volume is drifting downward from $42K to $33K monthly against a $452K annual ceiling, indicating that even the active half of the procurement architecture is losing transactional gravity — Ariba Buying and Invoicing is being bypassed for some spend categories, weakening three-way matching coverage and compliance enforcement across the AP cycle.
Stand up a Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement activation workshop to identify a first production workload tied to Ariba data extension or finance integration, converting the dormant platform contract into an enabler for the procurement suite already in flight.
Run a sourcing-to-buying linkage review on Ariba Sourcing to migrate upcoming RFx events onto the platform and feed awarded outcomes directly into Ariba Buying catalogs, restoring the closed-loop governance the architecture was designed to enforce.

Land O'Lakes Inc.
YTD Consumed: $563K
YTD Budget: $674K
YTD ACV: $836K
Projected Consumed: $1.4M
Projected Budget: $1.6M
Projected ACV: $2.0M
Industry Insights
Cargill leads this agribusiness cohort in transactional volume and operational scale — IBP at $4.26M projected, Ariba Buying at $3.86M, Integration Suite at 106.7% — but ranks last in governance and orchestration maturity with Ariba Sourcing at 16%, Ariba Contracts at 45% declining, and Technology Foundation at $0, meaning the largest operator is scaling commodity transactions without the sourcing controls and contract enforcement that protect margins in volatile agricultural markets.
Corteva and CHS share an identical architectural anti-pattern — both have invested in foundational data and integration layers (Datasphere, Integration Suite, IBP) while leaving the intelligence and experience tiers dormant (SAC BI at 0.2% and 7.6% respectively, SAP Build at 26% and near-zero, Spend Analysis and Catalog collapsed) — which structurally blocks the seed-to-shelf traceability and integrated demand sensing that differentiate digitally mature agribusiness operators from transactional laggards.
Coop Regions operates as the smallest footprint in the cohort ($220K ACV) yet mirrors the same 'integration-active, planning-dormant' pattern seen at CHS and Corteva — Integration Suite at or above plan while Datasphere trails by $15,779, SAC Planning gaps $2,500, and Joule Studio sits at $0 — confirming that across this agribusiness peer group the connective middleware is provisioned but the closed-loop planning capabilities that convert integration into margin optimization remain universally unrealized.
VAT should execute a coordinated BTP platform rationalization across Cargill, Corteva, and CHS — consolidating fragmented CPEA entitlements, redirecting the combined ~$600K of stranded Technology Foundation and Build capacity into Joule, Datasphere, and SAC Planning workloads, and establishing a shared activation playbook that converts platform investment into the AI-enabled demand sensing and process automation agribusiness margin pressures require.
VAT should run an Ariba source-to-pay remediation workshop for Cargill and a parallel Spend Analysis activation for Corteva to restore the upstream sourcing, contracts, and category intelligence layers that are dormant while downstream buying and invoicing volumes breach contract ceilings — directly addressing the $3.86M and $1.4M procurement flows currently executing without negotiated-savings governance.
VAT should engage Land O'Lakes immediately to establish an enterprise architecture baseline and consumption profile, as the absence of any EA insight for a customer in a $10.9M ACV portfolio creates a blind spot that prevents accurate peer benchmarking and risks undetected architectural drift against the cohort's emerging patterns.

Tenneco Automotive
YTD Consumed: $596K
YTD Budget: $780K
YTD ACV: $966K
Projected Consumed: $1.4M
Projected Budget: $1.8M
Projected ACV: $2.3M
Industry Insights
Tenneco Automotive stands alone in this single-customer automotive cohort with a critical architectural failure: despite contracting ~$966K ACV across HCM, CX, SCM, Finance, and Data & AI, its Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement shows $0 consumption across all five LoB tenants, meaning this tier-1 automotive supplier is operating disconnected SaaS islands rather than the integrated intelligent enterprise required for automotive supply chain velocity, demand sensing, and OEM compliance.
Tenneco exhibits a 'foundation-starves-downstream' pathology that is particularly damaging for automotive suppliers — Employee Central under-population cascades into 83% flatlined Recruiting and 12-15% Onboarding/Career Development, while dormant BTP starves analytics and partial IBP (frozen at single-module $1,826/month) prevents the integrated S&OP capabilities that automotive OEMs increasingly mandate from tier-1 suppliers for just-in-sequence delivery and capacity visibility.
Tenneco's Integration Suite runs at only 24% YTD attainment with erratic project-driven spikes while CPEA integration entitlements sit fully dormant, which directly blocks the master data flows between EC, C4C, and IBP needed to close lead-to-cash and plan-to-deliver loops — a structural gap that exposes the company to automotive-specific risks including EDI compliance failures, ASN accuracy penalties, and inability to respond to OEM demand volatility.
VAT should commission an enterprise-wide BTP activation review across all five Tenneco CPEA tenants to designate named technical owners and consolidate the dormant platform entitlement into a governed integration substrate, as this single intervention unlocks the consumption potential across every contracted LoB and addresses the $1.16M gap between budget and consumed.
VAT should facilitate a cross-domain integration architecture workshop for Tenneco anchored on Integration Suite to design EC-to-C4C-to-IBP master data and event flows, specifically targeting automotive-critical capabilities including OEM EDI integration, demand signal processing, and supplier collaboration portals that justify the contracted but unconsumed platform investment.
VAT should establish a foundation-first adoption governance framework with Tenneco that gates downstream module activation on demonstrated foundation readiness in Employee Central, BTP, and Integration Suite, preventing continued investment in starved downstream capabilities and redirecting the 38% consumption-to-ACV ratio toward the 62% dormant entitlement.

Industry Insights
H B Fuller Company is the only observable customer in this Chemicals cohort with enterprise-architecture visibility, yet its BTP platform sits at 0–9% attainment while Integration Suite is declining 14%, meaning the foundational integration fabric required for clean-core specialty chemicals digital supply chain transformation is architecturally dormant — Stepan Company and The Lubrizol Corporation lack any EA signal, placing them at unknown maturity but positioning H B Fuller as the only measurable reference point for peer comparison.
H B Fuller demonstrates that Chemicals companies can execute coherent multi-module architecture when ownership exists — its Concur T&E chain (Analytics, ExpenseIt, Extract at 110–123% attainment) proves end-to-end adoption is achievable, while its Procure-to-Pay spine remains incomplete (Ariba Contracts at $0, Buying at 15%), signaling that the higher-value direct/indirect spend optimization critical to chemicals margin management is architecturally unsupported and represents a capability gap the entire cohort likely shares.
The $6M budget against $1.9M consumed (31% consumption rate) across this Chemicals portfolio signals systemic underutilization — with H B Fuller's combined ~$45K BTP platform envelope trending toward shelfware and Stepan Company and The Lubrizol Corporation providing zero architectural visibility, the VAT lacks the adoption telemetry needed to drive consumption across 69% of uncommitted budget in an industry where integration-intensive batch manufacturing, formulation management, and regulatory compliance demand active platform investment.
Conduct immediate EA discovery engagements with Stepan Company and The Lubrizol Corporation to establish architectural baselines — the absence of any adoption signal for two-thirds of this cohort by customer count prevents peer benchmarking and masks consumption risk across the $6M portfolio.
Position H B Fuller's Concur T&E operating model (named ownership, embedded analytics, 110%+ attainment) as the replicable architectural blueprint for activating its dormant Ariba Contracts and BTP integration workloads, and use this as a proof point when engaging Stepan and Lubrizol on spend management and integration architecture.
Launch a Chemicals cohort BTP activation campaign targeting the clean-core integration patterns essential for batch process manufacturing, formulation IP protection, and REACH/EPA regulatory compliance — starting with H B Fuller's $45K dormant platform envelope and extending the value narrative to Stepan and Lubrizol once EA baselines are established.

Tropicana Products, Inc.

Industry Insights
S.C. Johnson leads this CPG cohort on data monetization with SAP Business Data Cloud running at 270% of plan, while Conagra, Tropicana, and Ingredion have contracted identical data foundations (Datasphere, Database Cloud CPEAs) that sit dormant or severely under-consumed — S.C. Johnson has operationalized data as a competitive asset while peers are paying for data architectures they cannot yet exploit for demand sensing, margin optimization, or supply chain visibility.
BTP platform foundation is the single most pervasive architectural gap across this consumer products portfolio — Conagra, Tropicana, Lancaster Colony, S.C. Johnson, Ingredion, and Domino's all show Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreements or Integration Suite entitlements at zero or declining consumption, meaning six of seven customers with EA data have contracted for clean-core extensibility and AI-readiness but are operationally running SAP as a legacy system of record, blocking the Joule-enabled, event-driven architectures CPG companies need for new product introduction speed and real-time trade promotion response.
Domino's and Tropicana share an identical broken-spine architecture where Integration Suite is the failing connective tissue — both show declining iFlow consumption while upstream data layers (HANA Cloud, Datasphere) and downstream execution layers (IBP, Business Network, Concur) atrophy in parallel, making them the two customers most at risk of complete cross-domain process fragmentation; by contrast, Conagra's Integration Suite runs at 175% of plan, demonstrating that integration fabric activation is achievable within this cohort when prioritized.
Launch a coordinated BTP Platform Foundation Activation Sprint across Conagra, Tropicana, Lancaster Colony, Ingredion, and Domino's — convene joint architecture sessions to land first production workloads on dormant Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreements and Integration Suite entitlements, using S.C. Johnson's BDC success pattern as the reference architecture for connecting data, planning, and execution domains.
Conduct a Source-to-Pay-to-Profitability Recovery Workshop targeting Conagra and Ingredion simultaneously — both show identical patterns of Ariba Spend Analysis generating insights that never reach dormant Buying/Invoicing or Profitability and Performance Management layers, representing a combined $700K+ of unrealized procurement and margin value that can be recovered by closing the analytical-to-transactional loop.
Establish Domino's and Tropicana as an Integration Suite Remediation Cohort with a shared playbook — both require the same architectural intervention (rebuilding dormant iFlows to reconnect IBP, Business Network, Concur, and Datasphere pipelines), and running parallel engagements will accelerate consumption recovery while generating reusable patterns for the broader CPG portfolio.

Industry Insights
Eli Lilly leads this life sciences cohort in contracted SAP footprint breadth (Ariba, IBP, SuccessFactors, Commerce, Signavio, SAC, BDC) but ranks last in platform activation discipline — Integration Suite consumption is down 54% and SAC Planning sits at 1.8% attainment while advisory wrappers run at 114–195%, meaning Lilly has the most sophisticated architecture on paper and the widest execution gap in practice, directly threatening the closed-loop pharma value chain from clinical trial through batch release to commercial fulfillment.
Abbott Laboratories is the only customer in this cohort with a functioning integration backbone (Integration Suite at 107%+) and active data ingestion layer (Data Intelligence Cloud at 99%), placing it architecturally ahead of AbbVie (BTP/Integration Suite at 0%), Cardinal Health (Ariba dormant, Build sporadic), Hill-Rom (no integration spine identified), and Eli Lilly (Integration Suite declining) — yet Abbott's failure to land data into HANA Cloud (65%) or monetize it through SAP Build (40%) means the cohort's integration leader still cannot deliver the closed-loop regulated-manufacturing operating model its medical device business requires.
GxP compliance automation is a shared systemic risk across the pharma/medical device cohort — AbbVie's Batch Release Hub is dormant with $575K Traceability Hub capacity unconsumed, Eli Lilly's Batch Release Hub shows zero activity despite IBP running at capacity, and Abbott lacks integrated planning-to-quality workflows entirely — leaving all three exposed to DSCSA, EU FMD, and electronic batch disposition mandates while Cardinal Health and Hill-Rom, as distributors and device manufacturers respectively, inherit downstream serialization and supplier qualification gaps from this upstream fragmentation.
Convene a cross-cohort BTP and Integration Suite activation sprint targeting AbbVie ($82K shelfware at 0% consumption), Cardinal Health (Build at 68% YTD with no governed backlog), and Eli Lilly (Integration Suite down 54%) to establish the enterprise integration fabric all three need for GxP automation, supplier-to-product traceability, and closed-loop pharma compliance workflows.
Run a pharma compliance architecture workshop jointly with AbbVie and Eli Lilly pairing their dormant Batch Release Hub capacity with active Traceability Hub and IBP investments to scope end-to-end electronic batch disposition and serialization workflows before DSCSA and EU FMD regulatory exposure compounds across both accounts.
Initiate a portfolio-wide advisory-to-activation rebalancing review across Eli Lilly (Preferred Success at 114–195% vs. platforms at 1.8–44%), Hill-Rom (Preferred Success Commerce at 119–120% vs. dormant Sourcing/Contracts), and Abbott (design layers mature, execution layers lagging) to convert over-consumed guidance entitlements into funded platform activation tracks tied to named LoB process owners rather than standalone advisory consumption.

Projected ACV: $516K
Industry Insights
Cleveland-Cliffs operates as the sole customer in this Mining industry cohort with a fragmented multi-domain SAP estate — IBP, C4C, SAC, and Integration Suite are all provisioned but running as isolated single-workload tools — resulting in a 45% consumption rate ($118,904 consumed against $215,011 ACV) that represents significant shelfware risk and blocks the integrated order-to-plan-to-cash visibility critical for commodity steel margin defense against import pricing pressure and decarbonization cost headwinds.
Integration Suite at Cleveland-Cliffs is the architectural root cause of cross-domain dysfunction — operating at $27/month against a $1,706 budget — which directly explains why C4C remains a CRM island without ERP closure, IBP demand/supply modules sit dormant, and SAC is confined to descriptive BI, leaving approximately $88,000+ in combined FY2026 under-consumption as evidence of an integrated enterprise that was contracted but never assembled.
Cleveland-Cliffs has no architectural path to AI-driven decisioning — SAC lacks Joule, planning, or predictive layers while IBP runs episodically — which positions this integrated steelmaker behind where the industry needs to be on real-time margin optimization, demand sensing, and mix decisioning capabilities essential for navigating volatile scrap pricing and customer allocation decisions in North American steel markets.
Convene a cross-domain integration architecture review for Cleveland-Cliffs spanning Integration Suite, IBP, C4C, and S/4HANA to design the iFlows that close the order-to-plan-to-cash loop, redirecting the $1,386 unconsumed Integration Suite budget into governed B2B, EDI, and event-driven scenarios that connect customer signals to production planning.
Execute a joint IBP-SAC-Joule activation workshop for Cleveland-Cliffs to extend SAC from BI-only into integrated planning bound to IBP's demand and supply modules, establishing a named S&OP cadence that converts the flat $19,419 IBP baseline into a unified analytics-and-planning platform aligned to steel industry margin resilience.
Establish a Cleveland-Cliffs enterprise consumption and entitlement baseline across all contracted domains — C4C, SAC, IBP, and Integration Suite — to expose shelfware risk where contract visibility is absent and build a sequenced activation roadmap that closes the $96,000+ gap between ACV and consumed value through steel-industry-specific use cases.

Industry Insights
Marathon Petroleum Company LP shows a consumption efficiency of only 39% ($767,104 consumed against $1,959,834 budget), indicating significant architectural dormancy that, without EA insights available, suggests either stalled S/4HANA modernization or underutilized platform capabilities critical for downstream refining optimization and hydrocarbon accounting.
As the sole customer in this oil, gas, and energy cohort with visible financials, Marathon Petroleum cannot be peer-ranked internally, but industry benchmarks indicate midstream and downstream operators at this ACV level ($1M+) typically require active BTP integration for real-time terminal operations, blend optimization, and emissions tracking—capabilities that remain unvalidated due to missing architectural documentation.
The absence of enterprise-architecture insights for Marathon Petroleum represents a shared risk pattern seen across energy sector accounts where legacy SCADA/OT integration complexity and refinery-specific customizations obscure true SAP footprint visibility, leaving the VAT unable to identify high-value consumption drivers like predictive maintenance or margin optimization.
VAT should immediately conduct an architectural discovery workshop with Marathon Petroleum to map their SAP landscape across refining, logistics, and trading operations, establishing baseline maturity and unlocking the $1.2M consumption gap.
VAT should position a BTP integration assessment for Marathon Petroleum focused on hydrocarbon movement and inventory reconciliation—a critical capability for downstream operators where real-time data from terminals and pipelines drives both regulatory compliance (EPA/RFS reporting) and margin optimization.
VAT should benchmark Marathon Petroleum against energy industry peers outside this portfolio (e.g., Valero, Phillips 66) to create competitive urgency around digital refinery and sustainability reporting capabilities that justify accelerated platform consumption.

VS Service Company, LLC

YTD Budget: $299K
YTD ACV: $257K
Projected Consumed: $663K
Projected Budget: $711K
Projected ACV: $616K
Industry Insights
VS Service Company, LLC stands alone in this Professional Services cohort with a structurally inverted CPEA profile — over-consuming in Technology Foundation (Joule Studio at 133%, Integration Suite at 113%) and Data & AI while leaving Finance/Spend Management and BDC Public entirely dormant — a pattern that in billable-hours businesses fragments the critical link between project delivery systems and procurement governance, eroding the margin visibility and rate realization that define Professional Services profitability.
VS Service Company, LLC has activated transactional execution layers (Ariba Buying/Invoicing, Business Data Cloud, Integration Suite) but abandoned upstream governance (Ariba Sourcing at 29%, BDC Public at 9.5%, Technology Foundation Other at 0%), which for a Professional Services firm means subcontractor and vendor spend flows through without competitive sourcing discipline — a direct leak on project margins in an industry where third-party cost pass-through is often the difference between profitable and underwater engagements.
The portfolio shows a $27K+ projected overage against ~$42K of unconsumed capacity in the same contract envelope, meaning VS Service Company, LLC is simultaneously paying penalty rates on hot workloads and carrying shelfware on cold ones — a mis-allocation pattern that, unaddressed, will compound into both fiscal exposure and audit risk as the agreement matures.
Convene a cross-domain CPEA rebalancing session with VS Service Company, LLC to true-up overage on Joule Studio, Integration Suite, and Database Cloud against the dormant BDC Public and Finance CPEA balances, converting fiscal exposure into legitimate consumption before year-end close.
Deliver an Ariba-to-BDC integration workshop for VS Service Company, LLC that wires Sourcing and Buying/Invoicing data into SAP Business Data Cloud via Integration Suite, activating the dormant Finance CPEA while establishing closed-loop source-to-pay governance essential for Professional Services margin control.
Assign named LoB owners and define first production use cases for the inert Technology Foundation Other, BDC Public, and Finance CPEA scopes at VS Service Company, LLC to convert contracted-but-unlaunched capacity into active adoption rather than expiring shelfware.

Target Corporation
YTD Consumed: $2.6M
YTD Budget: $2.4M
YTD ACV: $3.1M
Projected Consumed: $6.2M
Projected Budget: $5.8M
Projected ACV: $7.5M
Industry Insights
Target Corporation stands alone in this single-customer cohort but benchmarks poorly against retail industry norms: 83% of contracted ACV remains unconsumed ($2.58M of $3.1M), with the entire intelligent-enterprise foundation — BDC at $0, CPEA at $0, Build at 15% — dormant while transactional edges (Ariba Buying 121%, Concur 105-130%, Integration Suite 129%) over-consume, meaning Target is running high-volume retail operations without the data governance, AI enablement, or planning capabilities that define leading retailers' competitive advantage in demand sensing and inventory optimization.
Target's architectural pattern — active spend execution with absent spend governance — creates a retail-specific risk: Ariba Buying at 121% and Concur at 130% without Ariba Contracts (67%) or Multi Bank Connect (15%) activation means supplier payments and employee expenses flow at scale with no upstream contract compliance enforcement or downstream treasury settlement automation, leaving margin leakage undetected in an industry where basis-point improvements in procurement efficiency directly impact thin retail margins.
The Preferred Success SCM advisory consumption at 113% with zero IBP, EWM, or TM deployment represents the most architecturally incoherent investment in Target's portfolio — the retailer is paying for supply chain strategic guidance while having no SAP supply chain execution capability live, which for a retailer competing on omnichannel fulfillment and inventory visibility against Amazon and Walmart means the advisory value cannot translate into operational differentiation.
VAT should escalate a BDC + CPEA activation engagement as the single highest-priority intervention, positioning the $0 consumption against Target's stated AI and data strategy and framing activation as the prerequisite for every downstream capability — demand forecasting, supplier risk sensing, merchandising intelligence — that differentiates winning retailers.
VAT should structure a P2P closed-loop workshop connecting Ariba Buying overage to Ariba Contracts under-adoption and Multi Bank Connect dormancy, demonstrating to Target procurement and treasury leadership how governed sourcing-to-settlement flows recover margin currently lost to maverick spend and manual bank reconciliation.
VAT should condition continued Preferred Success SCM investment on a concrete IBP or EWM deployment commitment, presenting Target leadership with a choice: convert advisory consumption into live supply chain planning and execution capability, or acknowledge the advisory spend is stranded without architectural follow-through.

Border States Industries, Inc.
YTD Consumed: $410K
YTD Budget: $454K
YTD ACV: $399K
Projected Consumed: $984K
Projected Budget: $1.1M
Projected ACV: $957K
Industry Insights
Border States Industries is the sole customer in this wholesale distribution cohort and establishes the baseline architectural pattern: a structurally complete SAP enterprise stack (BTP CPEA, Commerce Cloud, SuccessFactors, Concur) operating as disconnected systems of record rather than an integrated distribution platform, with over-consumption on integration plumbing (Integration Suite at 114%, Joule Studio at 208%) masking under-activation of the customer-facing and talent modules (Commerce at 32%, SF Compensation at 0%, Career Development at 41%) that drive distributor margin expansion and workforce retention.
Border States exhibits a single-point-of-failure integration architecture where one under-governed BTP Integration Suite underpins finance close, T&E automation, and HR-to-payroll flows—a critical risk for wholesale distributors where order-to-cash velocity and workforce productivity directly determine competitive position, and the May Concur Expense collapse signals this fragility is already manifesting in production.
The $260K+ annual contracted capacity sitting dormant or misallocated at Border States represents a wholesale distribution anti-pattern: paying for AI-extensibility and data platform capabilities (Data & AI at 113%) without wiring them into the Commerce B2B self-service, dynamic pricing, or inventory optimization use cases that define digital distribution leadership—leaving ROI capped on the very capabilities that differentiate high-performing distributors.
Establish Border States as the wholesale distribution reference architecture by convening a cross-domain BTP governance council to reallocate the Data & AI and Joule Studio overrun toward Datasphere/SAC workloads that feed Commerce pricing analytics, Concur spend visibility, and SuccessFactors workforce planning—converting integration spend into distribution-specific outcome metrics.
Execute an integration-chain assurance review at Border States spanning Concur Expense→ERP, SuccessFactors EC→Payroll→Compensation, and Commerce→Order Management to diagnose the May Concur collapse, certify integration resilience, and remove the single-point-of-failure blocking activation of dormant talent and commerce modules.
Launch an enterprise value-realization roadmap at Border States to sequence activation of SF Compensation, SF Career Development, and Commerce B2B self-service against the ~$120K aggregate annual shortfall, targeting measurable wholesale distribution outcomes: digital order share growth, margin improvement through pricing intelligence, and talent retention in a labor-constrained logistics market.
